Every listed path is a .jsx file inside app/canvases/workshop/
The component names are AIEngine, Blocks, Export, Layouts, Library, and Polish
The file contains only location metadata; no code text, imports, or symbols
The ledger path encodes a wave/alloc/batch hierarchy: wave-002/alloc/batch-007
standouts: The batch is entirely composed of workshop canvas JSX components
The names suggest a design-tool pipeline: AI engine, blocks, layouts, library, polish, export
There are no code symbols or content, only file-location records
conventions: Uniform object shape across all entries: project, path, root
Relative source path paired with an absolute root directory
Batch organized under a wave/alloc/batch directory naming scheme
JSON array used as a simple manifest
concerns: No explanation of why these six files are grouped together
No code content or symbols, so interpretation must rely on external file reads
Absolute root path is user-specific and may not be portable
notes: A batch allocation manifest pointing to six workshop canvas JSX components in claude-ds for interpretive processing.

purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the path suggests an interpretive allocation batch, and the content is exactly a list of source-file locations to be interpreted.
apparent_themes: Separation of file-location metadata from source content
intention_signals: The file appears to be creating a structured manifest of workshop canvas components so an interpretive pipeline can process them together as batch 007 of wave 002.
novelty: It is a pure location manifest with no embedded code or symbol data, delegating content resolution to the paths it records.
contribution: It contributes a normalized, machine-readable batch manifest that maps interpretive work to exact project paths, supporting downstream fusion and querying without requiring content to be inlined.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON batch manifest listing six JSX component files under app/canvases/workshop in the claude-ds project, each with project name, relative path, and absolute root.
intention_for_embedding: To allocate a specific set of workshop canvas source files for interpretive analysis in wave 002, batch 007.
